title: O Captain My Captain by Walt Whitman | Summary in Hindi
description:
When Abraham Lincoln was assassinated in April 1865, Walt Whitman — who had spent the war years nursing wounded soldiers — channelled his grief into an elegy so powerful it became one of the most quoted poems in American history. "O Captain! My Captain!" uses an extended metaphor of a ship's captain to mourn Lincoln's death just as the Civil War's storm had passed, making themes of sacrifice and loss devastatingly personal for BA English Honours and DU SOL students studying Whitman's poetry. The poem's haunting rhythm will stay with you long after the explanation ends.
